query: We pin every dependency, commit our lockfiles, and Dependabot keeps our security alerts at zero. So dependency management is handled — we don't need to touch versions unless a CVE forces us to, right?
expected_behavior: Pushes back: pinning + locking guarantees reproducibility, not that the pinned version stays good — an unbumped pin rots (drifts toward end-of-life, misses non-security bug/perf fixes, compounds into a painful multi-major upgrade later)

Names the reproducibility-vs-freshness tension explicitly and prescribes a proactive currency check on a cadence, separate from the security audit (pip list --outdated / npm outdated / brew outdated / mas outdated report-only / Dependabot or Renovate version-updates for Actions pins and base-image digests)
Distinguishes the two lanes: a security bump is urgent (alert-to-zero) while a freshness bump is scheduled, batched, and deliberate

Keeps the freshness upgrade safe: reviewed as code, run through the thin contract test so a breaking upgrade fails red, and held behind a release-age cooldown (Renovate minimumReleaseAge) so a freshly-published malicious version can't reach you immediately
Flags that a runtime/library/app past end-of-support is a security-floor issue, not just hygiene
anti_behavior: Agrees that pinning + zero security alerts is sufficient and version currency can be ignored until a CVE forces an upgrade
Recommends blindly auto-merging update PRs or chasing latest with no cooldown or contract test
Recommends running mas upgrade in automation
